title: Manager, HR Data & Analytics
description:
Our client is actively searching for a Manager, HR Data & Analytics to join their team!

If you are a dynamic individual seeking a new career opportunity, read further!

Reporting to the Human Resource Technology team, you will harness data to support HR operations and help transform data into actionable insights that drive strategic decision-making within the University.

The responsibilities of this role are:

HR Reporting

  • Develop, maintain, and distribute regular HR reports, dashboards, and key performance indicators (KPIs) to track HR metrics such as turnover, recruitment efficiency and workforce data/trends to measure the effectiveness of processes and overall organizational health.
  • Support reporting requests for regulatory purposes.
  • Ensure accuracy and timeliness of data reporting and address any discrepancies
  • Work with internal stakeholders to harness HR Analytics in supporting HR operation. This may include analysing complex datasets to present useful manpower data for analysis and reporting

Data Management

  • Analyze and clean HR data from various sources, ensuring data integrity and confidentiality.
  • Implement data governance practices to maintain high-quality data standards.

HR Analytics Projects

  • Drive HR analytics projects aimed at enhancing recruitment processes, employee retention strategies, and overall workforce planning
  • Collaborate with HR leaders to identify areas for improvement and propose actionable insights based on data analysis.
  • Provide training and support to HR staff on data tools and analytics processes.

The ideal candidate will need to have the following qualities and experience:

  • Degree in Business Analytics, Statistics, Human Resources or equivalent
  • At least 8 - 10 years of relevant experience in HR function, with at least 5 years in a role that manages reporting/ analytics work.
  • Experience with Workday Report Writer, Power BI, Qlik Sense and Microsoft Excel (familiarity with statistical packages and/or SQL a plus) is preferred.
  • Excellent problem-solving capabilities and strong analytical and critical thinking ability. Deep interest and aptitude in data metrics and trends
  • Ability to work with senior stakeholders to translate data into meaningful information for supporting manpower and recruitment strategies and planning
  • Knack for presenting data in an easy-to-understand manner
  • Proven ability to multi-task and manage multiple projects
  • Excellent communication skills

All applicants' CV will be treated with the strictest confidentiality. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

CTES Consulting PTE LTD

postedCompany.description:
CTES CONSULTING PTE. LTD.

Our Recruitment Consultants are equipped with extensive recruitment and industry expertise in their

areas of specialisation.

 

The experience combined with their commitment to establish a deep understanding of our client and

their business priorities enable CTES to deliver value far beyond the service provided by traditional

search firms.

 

We provide an impeccable match. At CTES, we believe in providing a seamless match between

candidate and client. We deem it our responsibility to thoroughly understand our client's needs, and to

obtain in-depth knowledge of the job requirements and the culture of the client's organization. This

enables us to provide suitable and proficient candidates to our clients.

 

By ensuring a close match between the candidate and position, we are able to foster a win-win

situation, for both our clients and our candidates.

 

CTES aims to be an organisation renowned throughout the Asia-Pacific region, integral to both

employers and career seekers. We hope to become the preferred HR and career consultants for our

clients and candidates respectively.
